mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls over a recent multi-month window, discount rates and financing costs ease, so equity valuations expand and the S&P 500 tends to post positive 20-day forward returns. The signal fails if rate cuts are not followed by broader easing in inflation or activity and equities continue to sell off.
failure_condition: If decreases in FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL inflation rate above the prior 3-month average indicates sticky inflation and likely tighter policy expectations, which compress equity multiples and push the S&P 500 lower over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or if the sign accuracy remains below 52% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth marks labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect weakens when unemployment is flat or payroll growth is still accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-slowdown episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the combined signal adds no incremental explanatory power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer demand and revenue support for firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-month spike.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate turns positive and exceeds its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST indicates improving real-side production and construction activity, which lowers recession risk and supports equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails if output gains occur without parallel housing recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if either proxy alone explains the effect better than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO drops below its recent trend, the market reads it as a manufacturing slowdown and earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal should be most reliable during broad industrial weakness rather than isolated monthly noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses significance after excluding one-month reversals.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE combined with still-strong PAYEMS growth signals durable labor income support and consumer spending resilience, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if unemployment is low but payrolls are contracting.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE at or below its 12-month average and PAYEMS 3-month change positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment/high-payroll condition is not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the interaction term is not statistically stronger than the marginal effects.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPIAUCSL signals policy pressure against inflation, which initially raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect weakens when inflation is already falling fast.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-hot-inflation periods fail to generate negative 60-day SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the sign flips in a rolling 3-year tightening subsample.
